extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a medium–high value (around 0.5–0.6) and quickly drops within the first 10 time steps to a low value (around 0.2 or below).
R2: In the middle section (roughly time 15–45) the series stays in a mid-range band, mostly between 0.3 and 0.6, with only moderate fluctuations.
R3: There is no very sharp, isolated spike in the middle (no single narrow peak clearly above ~0.7 around time 35–45).
R4: Towards the end (after time ~50) the series recovers to mid-level values (around 0.3–0.5) but without forming a dominant high peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a medium–high value (around 0.5–0.7) and also drops early, but not as deep or as flat as Class 0 (typically not staying long below ~0.15).
R2: In the middle section (roughly time 20–45) there is at least one pronounced peak that climbs to a high value (around 0.7–0.8), noticeably higher than the surrounding mid-level region.
R3: Around the late-middle to early-final part (roughly time 45–55) the series shows a deep dip (close to or below ~0.15) immediately followed by a strong rebound.
R4: In the final section (after time ~55) the series shows a clear rising segment or local maximum in the upper-mid range (around 0.5–0.6), making the tail look more prominently peaked than in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and decreases sharply within the first 10 time steps to below 0.3.  
R2: Contains a broad mid-section (roughly time 15–40) where values stay mostly between 0.3 and 0.6 with only gentle fluctuations.  
R3: Has a single pronounced peak in the mid-to-late part (around time 35–45) that reaches at least about 0.7.  
R4: Ends in the last 10–15 time steps at a moderate level (about 0.35–0.55), clearly above the earlier minimum but below the largest peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and decreases sharply within the first 10 time steps to below 0.3 (similar to class 0).  
R2: In the mid-section (roughly time 15–40), values oscillate more strongly than class 0, repeatedly moving between about 0.35 and 0.6 rather than forming a flat plateau.  
R3: In the late part (after about time 50) there is a clear “rise-and-hold”: the series rises from below 0.3 to above 0.5 and then stays around 0.5–0.6 with only small fluctuations.  
R4: Compared with its mid-section, the final 10–15 time steps are consistently higher on average (end segment mean clearly greater than mid-segment mean).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (above 0.55) and drops steeply to a low valley (below 0.25) within the first 10 time steps.  
R2: After this early valley, rises to a broad mid-level plateau (mostly between 0.35 and 0.55) around the middle (time steps ~20–40) without very sharp spikes.  
R3: In the last third, shows one moderate peak (around 0.45–0.55) but never returns to very high values (stays below about 0.65).  
R4: Overall has smoother medium-level middle section and less extreme late peaks than Class 1.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (above 0.55) and also drops steeply to a low valley (below 0.25) within the first 10 time steps, similar to Class 0.  
R2: Middle segment (~20–40) contains a clear pronounced peak that reaches or exceeds about 0.7, higher and sharper than in Class 0.  
R3: After the big middle peak, there is a deep dip (near or below 0.2) followed by a strong late rise to relatively high values (above about 0.55) near the end.  
R4: Overall shows a more “U-and-spike” structure: very high middle spike plus a strong late surge, making the late part higher and more energetic than in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply to a local minimum below about 0.25 within the first 10–12 time steps.  
R2: After this early dip, the mid‑segment (roughly time 15–35) stays in a moderate band, mostly between 0.3 and 0.55, without very sharp spikes.  
R3: Around time 35–45 there is a single clear peak where the value rises above about 0.6–0.7, then falls again.  
R4: In the last third of the series, values fluctuate modestly around 0.35–0.5 and do not show a new very sharp, tall peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply to a local minimum below about 0.25 within the first 10–12 time steps (similar to Class 0).  
R2: The mid‑segment (roughly time 15–35) again lies mostly in a moderate band between 0.35 and 0.55, but with several small oscillations rather than a smooth plateau.  
R3: In the region around time 40–50 there is a very pronounced, narrow spike where the value shoots above about 0.75 and then quickly returns below ~0.3.  
R4: After this tall spike, the last third of the series shows another noticeable rise to around 0.5–0.6, forming a secondary broad peak rather than staying flat.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply to a low valley (≈0.15–0.25) in the first 10 time steps.  
R2: After this early valley, the signal stays in a mid-range band (≈0.3–0.5) without forming a very high spike, roughly between time 10 and 40.  
R3: In the middle of the series there is at most a moderate peak (≤0.7) rather than an extreme one; the highest value is not sharply isolated.  
R4: Toward the end (after about time 50), the series recovers only to mid values (≈0.4–0.55) and remains relatively flat, without a pronounced tall peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and also drops steeply to a low valley (≈0.15–0.25) in the first 10 time steps.  
R2: After this early valley, there is a clear mid-series build-up with several humps, culminating in a pronounced, relatively sharp peak above ≈0.75 around the middle (time ≈35–40).  
R3: Following this tall mid-series peak, the signal drops again to near-minimum values (≈0.1–0.2) before time 50.  
R4: In the last third (after about time 50), the series climbs again and forms a noticeable late peak (≈0.5–0.65), higher and more pronounced than the ending segment of Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The first large peak (within the first 10% of the series) is high but noticeably lower on average than in class 1 (around mid–high range, not extreme high).
R2: After this first drop, the middle section (roughly 20–50% of the series) stays in a moderate band with smaller, smoother undulations and no very sharp tall spike.
R3: In the second half (after ~50% of the series), the series does not contain a tall, narrow spike that clearly exceeds all surrounding values; peaks there are moderate and relatively broad.
R4: The overall pattern is “one early rise then mostly moderate fluctuations”, without a distinctly dominant spike later in the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins with a strong early peak (within the first 10% of the series) that reaches very close to the maximum range.
R2: The middle portion (about 20–50% of the series) shows several moderate peaks, similar to class 0, but none exceed the later main spike.
R3: In the second half (after ~50% of the series), there is a prominent, tall, relatively narrow spike that is among the highest points of the whole series and stands out from its immediate neighbors.
R4: The overall pattern is “early peak, then moderate fluctuations, then one dominant late spike” that is clearly higher than nearby values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and quickly drops to a low region (≈0.15–0.25) within the first 10 time steps.  
R2: After this early dip, rises to a medium plateau (≈0.4–0.5) around the middle section, without a very sharp, tall spike.  
R3: In the late middle (around time 35–50), shows a single broad peak that does not exceed about 0.7.  
R4: Ends in the last quarter at a medium level (≈0.35–0.55) with only mild local fluctuations.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and quickly drops to a low region (≈0.15–0.25) within the first 10 time steps, similar to class 0.  
R2: In the middle region (≈time 20–40), stays around a medium level (≈0.4–0.5) with small oscillations, without a pronounced, isolated mid-peak.  
R3: In the late middle (≈time 40–55), exhibits a tall, sharp spike that clearly exceeds about 0.75.  
R4: After this spike, in the final segment, returns to a medium–high level (≈0.45–0.6) with more noticeable variability than class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts at a relatively high level (around 0.5–0.6) and quickly drops to a local minimum below 0.3 within the first 10–15% of the series.  
R2: After this early drop, the middle portion (roughly 20–50% of the series) stays in a mid-range band, mostly between 0.3 and 0.6, with only moderate fluctuations.  
R3: There is no single, very sharp dominant peak in the middle; any peaks in the central part stay below about 0.75 and are not extremely isolated.  
R4: The tail (last 20–30% of the series) returns to and remains in a mid-range level (about 0.3–0.5) without a late, large spike.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts at a relatively high level (around 0.6–0.7) and drops to a low value below 0.3, but this dip can be slightly later or more irregular than in Class 0.  
R2: Contains a pronounced, sharp peak in the middle third of the series (roughly 30–50% of the length) that clearly stands out and reaches near or above about 0.75.  
R3: Around this dominant central peak, the signal shows stronger up–down swings than in Class 0, with visible rapid changes between low (≈0.2) and high (≈0.6–0.8) values.  
R4: The tail (last 20–30% of the series) tends to climb again to moderately high values (around 0.45–0.6), often after a deep dip close to 0.1 near the late-middle region.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the early segment (indices ~0–15), after an initial rise, the series quickly drops to a low valley and then recovers only slowly, staying mostly in the lower half of the value range.  
R2: In the mid segment (indices ~15–45), the series has a broad, modest “plateau” with values around the middle (≈0.4–0.5) and without a very sharp tall peak.  
R3: In the late segment (indices ~45–70), the series dips again but its last part finishes around mid-level values (≈0.4–0.5) rather than forming a tall peak or staying very low.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the early segment (indices ~0–15), the series falls to a low valley and then rebounds more strongly, reaching mid-to-high values more quickly than class 0.  
R2: In the mid segment (indices ~15–45), there is a noticeable higher peak (around or above ≈0.7–0.8) that stands out more sharply than any peak in class 0.  
R3: In the late segment (indices ~45–70), after a deep drop, the series rises to a relatively tall late peak (around ≈0.5–0.6) before ending, instead of just leveling off at mid level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (above 0.6) and drops steeply to below 0.3 within the first ~10 time units.  
R2: In the middle segment (about time 15–40), the series stays relatively flat/plateaued between 0.35 and 0.55 without sharp spikes.  
R3: After this plateau, a single broad peak appears (up to about 0.7–0.8) followed by a smooth decline.  
R4: In the last third of the series, values remain moderate (roughly 0.25–0.5) without a very deep dip below 0.15.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (above 0.6) and also drops to below 0.3 in the first ~10 time units, similar to Class 0.  
R2: In the middle segment (about time 15–40), the series oscillates more, with noticeable up‑and‑down fluctuations rather than a flat plateau.  
R3: After this middle segment, there is a very sharp, narrow peak reaching around 0.8, followed by a very deep dip going close to 0.1.  
R4: In the last third of the series, there is a pronounced rise again to around 0.5–0.6, forming a clear secondary peak after the deep dip.
